root_text.an3.67:1.3: Atītaṁ vā, bhikkhave, addhānaṁ ārabbha kathaṁ katheyya:
root_text.an3.67:1.5: Anāgataṁ vā, bhikkhave, addhānaṁ ārabbha kathaṁ katheyya:
root_text.an3.67:1.7: Etarahi vā, bhikkhave, paccuppannaṁ addhānaṁ ārabbha kathaṁ katheyya:
root_text.an3.67:2.1: Kathāsampayogena, bhikkhave, puggalo veditabbo yadi vā kaccho yadi vā akacchoti.
root_text.an3.67:2.2: Sacāyaṁ, bhikkhave, puggalo pañhaṁ puṭṭho samāno ekaṁsabyākaraṇīyaṁ pañhaṁ na ekaṁsena byākaroti, vibhajjabyākaraṇīyaṁ pañhaṁ na vibhajja byākaroti, paṭipucchābyākaraṇīyaṁ pañhaṁ na paṭipucchā byākaroti, ṭhapanīyaṁ pañhaṁ na ṭhapeti, evaṁ santāyaṁ, bhikkhave, puggalo akaccho hoti.
root_text.an3.67:2.3: Sace panāyaṁ, bhikkhave, puggalo pañhaṁ puṭṭho samāno ekaṁsabyākaraṇīyaṁ pañhaṁ ekaṁsena byākaroti, vibhajjabyākaraṇīyaṁ pañhaṁ vibhajja byākaroti, paṭipucchābyākaraṇīyaṁ pañhaṁ paṭipucchā byākaroti, ṭhapanīyaṁ pañhaṁ ṭhapeti, evaṁ santāyaṁ, bhikkhave, puggalo kaccho hoti.
root_text.an3.67:3.1: Kathāsampayogena, bhikkhave, puggalo veditabbo yadi vā kaccho yadi vā akacchoti.
root_text.an3.67:3.2: Sacāyaṁ, bhikkhave, puggalo pañhaṁ puṭṭho samāno ṭhānāṭhāne na saṇṭhāti parikappe na saṇṭhāti aññātavāde na saṇṭhāti paṭipadāya na saṇṭhāti, evaṁ santāyaṁ, bhikkhave, puggalo akaccho hoti.
root_text.an3.67:3.3: Sace panāyaṁ, bhikkhave, puggalo pañhaṁ puṭṭho samāno ṭhānāṭhāne saṇṭhāti parikappe saṇṭhāti aññātavāde saṇṭhāti paṭipadāya saṇṭhāti, evaṁ santāyaṁ, bhikkhave, puggalo kaccho hoti.
root_text.an3.67:4.1: Kathāsampayogena, bhikkhave, puggalo veditabbo yadi vā kaccho yadi vā akacchoti.
root_text.an3.67:4.2: Sacāyaṁ, bhikkhave, puggalo pañhaṁ puṭṭho samāno aññenaññaṁ paṭicarati, bahiddhā kathaṁ apanāmeti, kopañca dosañca appaccayañca pātukaroti, evaṁ santāyaṁ, bhikkhave, puggalo akaccho hoti.
root_text.an3.67:4.3: Sace panāyaṁ, bhikkhave, puggalo pañhaṁ puṭṭho samāno na aññenaññaṁ paṭicarati na bahiddhā kathaṁ apanāmeti, na kopañca dosañca appaccayañca pātukaroti, evaṁ santāyaṁ, bhikkhave, puggalo kaccho hoti.
root_text.an3.67:5.1: Kathāsampayogena, bhikkhave, puggalo veditabbo yadi vā kaccho yadi vā akacchoti.
root_text.an3.67:5.2: Sacāyaṁ, bhikkhave, puggalo pañhaṁ puṭṭho samāno abhiharati abhimaddati anupajagghati khalitaṁ gaṇhāti, evaṁ santāyaṁ, bhikkhave, puggalo akaccho hoti.
root_text.an3.67:5.3: Sace panāyaṁ, bhikkhave, puggalo pañhaṁ puṭṭho samāno nābhiharati nābhimaddati na anupajagghati na khalitaṁ gaṇhāti, evaṁ santāyaṁ, bhikkhave, puggalo kaccho hoti.
root_text.an3.67:6.1: Kathāsampayogena, bhikkhave, puggalo veditabbo yadi vā saupaniso yadi vā anupanisoti.
root_text.an3.67:6.2: Anohitasoto, bhikkhave, anupaniso hoti, ohitasoto saupaniso hoti.
root_text.an3.67:6.3: So saupaniso samāno abhijānāti ekaṁ dhammaṁ, parijānāti ekaṁ dhammaṁ, pajahati ekaṁ dhammaṁ, sacchikaroti ekaṁ dhammaṁ.
root_text.an3.67:6.4: So abhijānanto ekaṁ dhammaṁ, parijānanto ekaṁ dhammaṁ, pajahanto ekaṁ dhammaṁ, sacchikaronto ekaṁ dhammaṁ sammāvimuttiṁ phusati.
root_text.an3.67:6.8: etadatthaṁ sotāvadhānaṁ, yadidaṁ anupādā cittassa vimokkhoti.

translation_text.an3.67:1.1: “There are, mendicants, these three topics of discussion.
translation_text.an3.67:1.4: ‘That is how it was in the past.’
translation_text.an3.67:1.6: ‘That is how it will be in the future.’
translation_text.an3.67:1.7: Or you might discuss the present:
translation_text.an3.67:1.8: ‘This is how it is at present.’
translation_text.an3.67:2.1: You can know whether or not an individual is competent to hold a discussion by seeing how they take part in a discussion.
translation_text.an3.67:2.2: When an individual is asked a question, if it needs to be answered categorically and they don’t answer it categorically; or if it needs analysis and they answer without analyzing it; or if it needs a counter-question and they answer without a counter-question; or if it should be set aside and they don’t set it aside, then that individual is not competent to hold a discussion.
translation_text.an3.67:2.3: When an individual is asked a question, if it needs to be answered categorically and they answer it categorically; or if it needs analysis and they answer after analyzing it; or if it needs a counter-question and they answer with a counter-question; or if it should be set aside and they set it aside, then that individual is competent to hold a discussion.
translation_text.an3.67:3.1: You can know whether or not an individual is competent to hold a discussion by seeing how they take part in a discussion.
translation_text.an3.67:3.2: When an individual is asked a question, if they’re not consistent about what their position is and what it isn’t; about what they propose; about speaking from what they know; and about the appropriate procedure, then that individual is not competent to hold a discussion.
translation_text.an3.67:3.3: When an individual is asked a question, if they are consistent about what their position is and what it isn’t; about what they propose; about speaking from what they know; and about the appropriate procedure, then that individual is competent to hold a discussion.
translation_text.an3.67:4.1: You can know whether or not an individual is competent to hold a discussion by seeing how they take part in a discussion.
translation_text.an3.67:4.2: When an individual is asked a question, if they dodge the issue; distract the discussion with irrelevant points; or display annoyance, hate, and bitterness, then that individual is not competent to hold a discussion.
translation_text.an3.67:4.3: When an individual is asked a question, if they don’t dodge the issue; distract the discussion with irrelevant points; or display annoyance, hate, and bitterness, then that individual is competent to hold a discussion.
translation_text.an3.67:5.1: You can know whether or not an individual is competent to hold a discussion by seeing how they take part in a discussion.
translation_text.an3.67:5.2: When an individual is asked a question, if they intimidate, crush, mock, or seize on trivial mistakes, then that individual is not competent to hold a discussion.
translation_text.an3.67:5.3: When an individual is asked a question, if they don’t intimidate, crush, mock, or seize on trivial mistakes, then that individual is competent to hold a discussion.
translation_text.an3.67:6.1: You can know whether or not an individual has what’s required by seeing how they take part in a discussion.
translation_text.an3.67:6.2: If they actively listen they have what’s required; if they don’t actively listen they don’t have what’s required.
translation_text.an3.67:6.3: Someone who has what’s required directly knows one thing, completely understands one thing, gives up one thing, and realizes one thing—
translation_text.an3.67:6.4: and then they experience complete freedom.
translation_text.an3.67:6.5: This is the purpose of discussion,
translation_text.an3.67:6.8: and listening well, that is, the liberation of the mind by not grasping.
translation_text.an3.67:7.4: they look for flaws in each other.
translation_text.an3.67:8.1: They rejoice together when their opponent
translation_text.an3.67:8.2: speaks poorly and makes a mistake,
translation_text.an3.67:8.4: but the noble ones don’t discuss like this.
translation_text.an3.67:9.1: If an astute person wants to hold a discussion
translation_text.an3.67:9.2: connected with the teaching and its meaning—
translation_text.an3.67:9.3: the kind of discussion that noble ones hold—
translation_text.an3.67:9.4: then that wise one should start the discussion,
translation_text.an3.67:10.1: knowing when the time is right,
translation_text.an3.67:11.1: or with a mind full of jealousy,
translation_text.an3.67:11.2: they’d speak from what they rightly know.
translation_text.an3.67:11.3: They agree with what was well spoken,
translation_text.an3.67:11.4: without criticizing what was poorly said.
translation_text.an3.67:12.1: They’d not persist in finding faults,
translation_text.an3.67:12.3: neither intimidating nor crushing the other,
translation_text.an3.67:13.2: for the sake of knowledge and clarity.
translation_text.an3.67:13.3: That’s how the noble ones consult,

variant_text.an3.67:1.8: evaṁ hoti etarahi paccuppannamaddhānan’ti → evaṁ etarahi paccuppannanti (bj, pts1ed, mr); evaṁ hoti etarahi paccuppannanti (sya-all, km)
variant_text.an3.67:2.2: ṭhapanīyaṁ pañhaṁ na ṭhapeti → thapanīyaṁ pañhaṁ na thapeti (mr)
variant_text.an3.67:3.2: aññātavāde → aññavāde (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed); aññātavāre (mr)
variant_text.an3.67:8.4: tadariyo kathanācare → tadariyo na kathaṁ vade (mr)
